title: Nation's Largest Pro-Life Group Bringing More Abortion to Oklahoma
author: James Silberman
orientation: James Silberman dissects Oklahoma SB834 and exposes National Right to Life and its Oklahoma affiliate, led by Tony Lauinger, as the driving force behind a bill that would re-legalize surgical abortion for rape and incest, repeal Oklahoma's ban on self-managed abortion, shield abortifacient contraception, and fully protect IVF practices that destroy embryos. The piece argues that the flagship 'pro-life' establishment is actually one of the largest impediments to abolition and calls for grassroots Christians to expose and confront them.

questions.question: Who was the man lobbying Oklahoma senators for SB834, and what organizations does he lead?
questions.answer: The lobbyist was Tony Lauinger, who serves as Vice President of National Right to Life and President of Oklahomans for Life—meaning the bill bringing surgical abortion back to Oklahoma is being pushed by the leaders of the country's flagship pro-life organization, not by Planned Parenthood or NARAL.
questions.quote: Tony is the Vice President of National Right to Life and the President of Oklahomans for Life.

questions.question: How would SB834 change Oklahoma law on surgical abortion in cases of rape and incest?
questions.answer: SB834 would re-legalize surgical abortion when the pregnancy results from rape or sexual assault reported to law enforcement, or incest of a minor reported to law enforcement, opening the door for abortion companies that had been forced to stop surgical abortions to resume them.
questions.quote: No person may “administer,” “prescribe,” “procure,” or “advise” an abortion for a woman “unless…the pregnancy is the result of: a. rape or sexual assault that has been reported to law enforcement, or b. incest of a minor that has been reported to law enforcement.”

questions.question: What does the article say about Attorney General Gentner Drummond's willingness to enforce Oklahoma's existing self-managed abortion ban?
questions.answer: Silberman reports that AG Drummond has shown no interest in enforcing 63-1-733 even though self-managed abortions in Oklahoma have more than tripled since Roe was overturned, and that neither Drummond nor his predecessor John O'Connor responded to Senator Warren Hamilton's request for a legal opinion on the statute.
questions.quote: Oklahoma Attorney General Gentner Drummond appears to have no interest in enforcing §63-1-733 even though self-managed abortions have more than tripled in Oklahoma following the overturn of *Roe v. Wade*

questions.question: Why does the author claim that hormonal contraceptives can cause abortions?
questions.answer: Hormonal birth control has three mechanisms—inhibiting ovulation, thickening cervical mucus, and thinning the endometrium—and the third is abortifacient because it prevents a newly conceived embryo from implanting, causing the embryo to be flushed out in the next menstrual cycle.
questions.quote: attempts to thin the endometrium so that a newly formed embryo cannot implant in the uterus. The embryo is then flushed out in the next menstrual cycle.

questions.question: What estimates does the article cite for embryos killed annually by hormonal contraceptives and IUDs in the United States?
questions.answer: Citing Dr. Bogomir M. Kuhar via Randy Alcorn's pamphlet, the article reports an estimated 834,000 to 4,174,000 embryos killed each year by the third mechanism in the birth control pill, plus 3,825,000 deaths attributed to IUDs, 1,200,000 to Depo-Provera, and 2,925,000 to Norplant.
questions.quote: Dr. Bogomir M. Kuhar estimates that between 834,000 and 4,174,000 embryos are killed in the United States each year by the third mechanism present in the birth control pill.

questions.question: How does SB834 treat in vitro fertilization, and why does the author consider that a problem?
questions.answer: SB834 grants blanket protection to any 'act related to in vitro fertilization' performed by a licensed provider, which the author argues protects the standard IVF practices of discarding 'low-grade' embryos, losing them to freezer malfunctions, and donating them to research—a death toll he says likely rivals the abortion industry.
questions.quote: SB834 protects IVF wholesale. “An act related to in vitro fertilization” includes discarding low-grade embryos, freezer malfunctions, and other forms of murder perpetrated by the IVF industry.

questions.question: What British IVF reporting does the article use to illustrate embryo loss in the IVF industry?
questions.answer: Between 1991 and 2011, British Department of Health data showed 94,090 IVF live births out of more than 3.1 million embryos created—roughly 33 embryos per live birth—with about 1.45 million discarded, 101,605 destroyed in research, and 764,311 frozen indefinitely.
questions.quote: 94,090 live births from IVF out more than 3.1 million embryos created; nearly 33 embryos created for every 1 live birth.

questions.question: Does the author believe any form of IVF could be morally acceptable to abolitionists?
questions.answer: He concedes that an IVF practice that creates only one embryo at a time and immediately transfers each embryo into the mother's uterus could survive a law abolishing abortion, though he notes there are still moral and theological concerns with even this 'ethical IVF.'
questions.quote: if they only ever created one embryo at a time with the intention of speedily transferring every embryo created into the uterus of his or her mother.

questions.question: Why, according to the article, do other pro-life leaders refuse to publicly criticize National Right to Life?
questions.answer: Silberman says staff at other major pro-life organizations who privately disagree with NRTL won't speak out because you don't pick a fight with the most influential leaders of the movement—the organization that controls access to big rallies and conferences.
questions.quote: you don’t pick a fight with the most influential leaders of the movement. You don’t pick a fight with the organization that is your ticket to big-time rallies and conferences.

questions.question: What does the article ask pro-life Christians to do with their giving habits?
questions.answer: It urges Christians to stop reflexively writing checks to any group calling itself pro-life and instead seriously examine what organizations like National Right to Life actually do with the money—because those dollars are funding wicked legislation like SB834 and opposing righteous bills like the Abolition of Abortion in Louisiana Act.
questions.quote: Most simply write a check to any group that calls themselves pro-life and puts a cute baby picture on a mailer.

questions.question: What concrete actions does the article ask readers to take to expose National Right to Life?
questions.answer: Readers are asked to share the article on social media (optionally tagging 10 pro-life friends), email or text it to 10 pro-life friends, and send it to 10 conservative media outlets such as publications, podcasters, radio shows, or influencers, with the goal of pushing the embedded video past 100,000 views.
questions.quote: Send this article to 10 conservative media outlets. This could be conservative publications podcasters, radio shows, social media pages, or any other influencers you can think of.

related.title: The Three Pro-Life Men Keeping Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Earlier Silberman piece naming Tony Lauinger and other Oklahoma pro-life leaders as obstacles to abolition—direct background for SB834.

related.title: Uncovered Letter Exposes Plan to Keep Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Documents the same NRTL/Oklahomans for Life network coordinating to block abolition, providing historical context for the SB834 lobbying effort.

related.title: Oklahomans for Life Asks Candidates to Oppose Abortion's Immediate Abolition
related.reason: Shows Lauinger's group explicitly pressuring candidates against abolition bills, the same dynamic now driving SB834.

related.title: National Right to Life Endorses Greg McCortney, the Senator Who Kept Abortion Legal in Oklahoma
related.reason: Another instance of National Right to Life backing politicians who blocked abolition in Oklahoma, reinforcing the article's central indictment of NRTL.

related.title: Rep. Alan Seabaugh and the Louisiana House Ensure Murder by Abortion Continues
related.reason: Covers the killing of the Abolition of Abortion in Louisiana Act referenced in the article as another NRTL-affiliated betrayal.

related.title: Weeping Time Documentary: The Beginning of an IVF Conversation
related.reason: Expands on the IVF death-toll critique that occupies a major section of the SB834 article.

related.title: Disgraceful! Pro-Life Establishment Lies About Abolitionist Candidate Warren Hamilton
related.reason: Profiles Senator Warren Hamilton, the abolitionist senator the article calls 'the champion of preborn children at the Oklahoma State capitol.'
